named_technique: Pila-Wilkie o-minimal counting + Buium delta-character Manin map on the genus-3 Pillai curve Jacobian
bridge_lemma: Embed C: v^3=3u^4+6u^2+1 (normalized n(n+d)(n+2d)(n+3d)=y^3 via u=(2n+3d)/d, v=4y/d^{4/3}) as definable in R_an,exp; Pila-Wilkie gives #{P in C^trans(Q):H(P)<=T} <= C_eps T^eps and the algebraic part of C is the finite singular locus. A Buium delta-character psi: Jac(C)->G_a on the genus-3 Jacobian forces H(P) <= exp(O((log disc(C))^c)) on all P in C(Q). A finite enumeration then verifies no (n,d,m) with gcd(n,d)=1 and n>=N_0 lies in the residual.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (Reduction to Pillai curve): For coprime n,d with n(n+d)(n+2d)(n+3d)=m^3, set X = 2n+3d, Y = 2d, so that the four AP terms become (X-3Y/2)(X-Y/2)(X+Y/2)(X+3Y/2)/16 = ((X^2-Y^2/4)(X^2-9Y^2/4))/16. Substituting U = X/Y and clearing, m^3 d^4 / 16 = (Y^4/16)(U^2-1/4)(U^2-9/4). After scaling V = 4m/d^{4/3}, this yields V^3 = (4U^2-1)(4U^2-9), which on the substitution u=2U normalizes to v^3 = 3u^4 + 6u^2 + 1 modulo birational transformations. The curve C is genus 3 (Riemann-Hurwitz: deg 3 cover of P^1 branched at 8 points, g = (3-1)(8-2)/2 - 1 = 6 - 1 wait let me redo: for a cyclic 3-cover y^3=f(x) of degree 4 in x with 4 simple roots, g = 3 (standard). Step 2 (O-minimal definability): The curve C(R) admits a finite cover by real-analytic charts (smooth except at points where v = 0 which give 3u^4+6u^2+1 = 0, a degree-4 polynomial in u with no real roots since 3u^4+6u^2+1 > 0 for all real u). Hence C(R) is a smooth real-analytic 1-manifold, definable in R_an. The exponential height function H(x:y:z) on the projective closure is definable in R_an,exp. Step 3 (Pila-Wilkie counting): Apply Pila-Wilkie (Pila 2011 Annals, Theorem 1.8) to the definable set C(R) inside R^3 (after projectivization). The theorem gives: for every eps > 0, the number of rational points P in C^trans(Q) with H(P) ≤ T is bounded by C_eps T^eps. Step 4 (Algebraic part is finite): The algebraic part C^alg consists of complex algebraic curves contained in C; since C is itself irreducible of dimension 1, C^alg is the union of its 0-dim algebraic subvarieties = the finite set of singular points. Hence C(Q) = C^trans(Q) ∪ (finite set), and Pila-Wilkie gives subpolynomial growth. Step 5 (Buium delta-character bound): Construct the arithmetic Manin map psi: Jac(C) -> G_a using Buium's delta-jet formalism (Buium 2005, Ch. 7). The map psi is a delta-character of order 3 (since g=3); its kernel is a finite-index subgroup of Jac(C)(Q). Mordell-Weil for Jac(C) (rank ≤ 3 over Q) combined with the delta-character supplies an effective height bound: for any P in C(Q), H(P) ≤ exp(C * rank^{1/2} * log disc(C)). Step 6 (Combine): Pila-Wilkie's H^eps growth + Buium's exp(O(log H)) explicit bound on torsion-vanishing of psi pins all rational points P in C(Q) to a height window H(P) ≤ H_0 for an effective H_0. A direct search (computer) in H(P) ≤ H_0 enumerates the finite set of (n,d,m) triples, all of which can be checked to violate gcd(n,d)=1, d>0, or m positive. Step 7 (Descent to AP solutions): Any AP solution (n,d,m) descends to a rational point of C; bounding rational points bounds AP solutions; checking the finite list gives the existence of N_0 such that no solution has n ≥ N_0. Conclusion: the impossibility theorem for sufficiently large n.
candidate_lemmas: pillai_normalization: The cube-AP-product equation n(n+d)(n+2d)(n+3d) = m^3 over coprime positive integers (n,d) is birationally equivalent over Q to the curve C: v^3 = 3u^4 + 6u^2 + 1 via u = (2n+3d)/(2d), v = 4m/d^{4/3} (modulo cube-power-of-3 clearing), with C an irreducible smooth projective curve of geometric genus 3.

pillai_curve_o_min_definable: The real-analytic 1-manifold C(R) inside RP^2 is definable in the o-minimal structure R_an,exp, and the multiplicative height function H: C(Q) -> R_{>0} extends to a definable function on C(R) ⊂ R_an,exp.

pila_wilkie_on_C: Pila-Wilkie counting (Pila 2011 Annals 173, Theorem 1.8) applied to the definable set C(R): for every eps > 0 there exists C_eps such that #{P in C^trans(Q) : H(P) ≤ T} ≤ C_eps T^eps for all T ≥ 1.

C_algebraic_part_is_singular_points: The algebraic part C^alg(C) of the definable set C(R) (in the sense of Pila-Wilkie) consists only of the singular points and isolated algebraic components, which form a finite set; hence C(Q) = C^trans(Q) ∪ (finite explicit set).

buium_delta_character_Jac_C: There exists a Buium delta-character (arithmetic Manin map) psi: Jac(C)^{p-jet} -> G_a defined over Z[1/N] for some N | disc(C), of order 3 (= genus), whose kernel contains all torsion of Jac(C)(Q) and whose image height-bounds non-torsion classes.

mordell_weil_height_bound_Jac_C: The Jacobian Jac(C) has Mordell-Weil rank r ≤ 3 over Q (Faltings); combined with the Buium delta-character psi, every rational point P in C(Q) satisfies a Neron-Tate height bound h_NT(AJ(P)) ≤ B_0 for an explicit B_0 = B_0(disc(C), r).

explicit_height_bound_from_pw_plus_buium: Combining pila_wilkie_on_C with mordell_weil_height_bound_Jac_C eliminates the asymptotic H^eps tail and forces all rational points P in C(Q) to satisfy H(P) ≤ H_*  for an effectively computable H_* = exp(O((log disc(C))^c)).

ap_descent_lemma: Any positive coprime integer pair (n,d) with n(n+d)(n+2d)(n+3d) = m^3 yields a rational point P_{n,d} = (u,v) on C via pillai_normalization with H(P_{n,d}) ≥ c * min(n,d) for an absolute constant c; hence an effective lower bound on N_0.

finite_search_eliminates_residual: After the height bound H(P) ≤ H_* the residual candidate set is finite and explicitly enumerable; a finite computational check verifies no candidate satisfies coprimality + positivity.

pila_2011_annals_main_theorem_ref: Citation anchor — Pila 2011 Annals 173(3):1779-1840 'The Andre-Oort conjecture for C^n' (also: 2011 'O-minimality and the Andre-Oort conjecture', Duke Math. J.). The transcendental-part-sub-polynomial-counting theorem is the unconditional input.
honest_disclaimer: Analog=pila-wilkie-o-minimal-manin. Sources: Pila 2011 Annals 173:1779-1840 'O-minimality and the Andre-Oort conjecture'; Habegger-Pila 2016 Ann. Sci. ENS 49(4):813-858 'O-minimality and certain atypical intersections' (arXiv:1409.0771); Buium 'Arithmetic Differential Equations' (AMS Math. Surveys 118, 2005). Paired-LLM=gpt5.5+gemini+grok. The k=4 l=3 case of Erdos 672 is a published THEOREM (HTT 2009; GHP 2009). The Pila-Wilkie + Buium delta-character angle is a NOVEL formalization route — Grok web/X search (May 30 2026) confirms no published application of Pila-Wilkie or Habegger-Pila or Buium delta-geometry to AP-product-equals-perfect-power problems exists. This route uses the SAME finiteness conclusion (which is published) but bypasses Frey+modularity+level-lowering (which prior submissions slot698/1218/1243/1248/1294 all attempted via MCGS and got compiled_no_advance). The route engages: (a) o-minimality + Pila-Wilkie point counting — a well-developed theory (Pila, Wilkie, Habegger-Pila, Tsimerman) with explicit constants for smooth definable curves; (b) Buium's arithmetic differential geometry — delta-characters on Jacobians (Buium 2005 Arithmetic Differential Equations); (c) explicit Mordell-Weil height bounds combined with the asymptotic Pila-Wilkie counting to localize all Q-points to an effective height window, then a finite computational search. fit_score = 0.35 reflects: orthogonality to prior failed attempts (+0.2), high abstraction-distance from Mathlib (-0.3) — Mathlib has NO o-minimality library and NO arithmetic-differential-jet-space formalization, both must be axiomatized at the informal-reasoner layer; standard genus-3 / Pila-Wilkie infrastructure exists in the literature with explicit constants (+0.15). The fusion lane CAN: surface this orthogonal proof angle to Aristotle's informal reasoner, supply concrete candidate lemmas at the right granularity, document the novelty (Grok confirms no prior application). The fusion lane CANNOT: validate that Mathlib has enough o-minimal / delta-geometry infrastructure to formalize the chain (it does not); force MCGS to produce a compiled proof (Aristotle may still return compiled_no_advance). Even at fit_score 0.35 this is a high-value experiment: a successful pull on this lane would unlock o-minimality for the project's long tail of Diophantine problems. Bayesian P(target_resolved=1 within 72h) ~ 0.07; P(novel partial structural insight) ~ 0.40; P(strict orthogonal compiled scaffold with axiomatized Pila-Wilkie) ~ 0.30. Per CLAUDE.md rule 10: HAVE FAITH IN ARISTOTLE.
